body:
## Problem
In the job detail page (`frontend/src/app/jobs/[id]/page.tsx`), after a `CREATE_JOB` on-chain transaction is broadcast and confirmed, the frontend sends the confirmation to the backend with `onChainJobId` hardcoded to `1`:

```typescript
await axios.post(`${API_URL}/escrow/confirm-tx`, {
  hash: txResult.hash,
  type: "CREATE_JOB",
  jobId: id,
  milestoneId,
  onChainJobId: 1, // ❌ hardcoded — every job gets contractJobId = "1"
}, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });
```

The backend stores `contractJobId: onChainJobId.toString()` on the `Job` record. With this hardcoded value, every single job initialized on-chain gets `contractJobId = "1"` in the database. When the backend later tries to fund or approve milestones for these jobs, it uses `contractJobId` to identify the job on-chain — so all subsequent escrow operations target job #1 regardless of which job is actually being worked on.

## Why This Matters
This completely breaks the escrow flow for any platform with more than one job. Fund and milestone approval transactions hit the wrong on-chain job.

## What Needs to Be Done

The correct `contractJobId` must be parsed from the transaction result. Stellar smart contract events or the transaction result XDR contain the on-chain job ID assigned during the `create_job` call.

```typescript
// After signAndBroadcastTransaction returns:
const txResult = await signAndBroadcastTransaction(res.data.xdr);

if (!txResult.success) throw new Error(txResult.error || "Transaction failed");

// Parse on-chain job ID from transaction result
// The contract emits a job_created event with the assigned job ID
// Use the Stellar RPC getTransaction response to extract it from resultXdr or events
const onChainJobId = parseJobIdFromResult(txResult); // implement this helper

await axios.post(`${API_URL}/escrow/confirm-tx`, {
  hash: txResult.hash,
  type: "CREATE_JOB",
  jobId: id,
  onChainJobId, // ✅ real value from the chain
}, { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });
```

The `parseJobIdFromResult` helper should decode the Stellar transaction result XDR or event data to extract the integer job ID returned by the contract.

## How to Verify
1. Create two jobs and initialize escrow for both
2. Check the database: both jobs should have different `contractJobId` values
3. Fund escrow for job #2 — it should target job #2 on-chain, not job #1

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `onChainJobId` is parsed from the actual transaction result, not hardcoded
- [ ] Each job's `contractJobId` in the database reflects its real on-chain ID
- [ ] Funding and milestone approval use the correct on-chain job ID
- [ ] If parsing fails, an informative error is shown to the user
title: Fix escrow confirm-tx hardcodes onChainJobId: 1 — every job stored with the same contract ID
